overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may not be liable for liquidated damages on early termination

overrides.override_summary: CA Business & Professions Code §§20000–20043 provides termination/non-renewal rights that supersede FA
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law provides additional protections against wrongful termination

overrides.override_summary: NY choice of law provision may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law may govern instead of NY law for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: VA venue requirement may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to litigate in CA rather than VA

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer excludes CA Franchise Investment Law and Franchise Relations Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot waive statutory franchise law rights

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve franchisor from liability under HI franchise law
overrides.why_it_matters: Statutory franchise claims preserved regardless of release

overrides.override_summary: Governing law, jurisdiction, venue, jury waiver do not waive rights under IL Franchise Disclosure Act. IL Act governs for IL licensees.
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees retain all IL statutory protections regardless of NY law clause

overrides.override_summary: Cannot waive compliance with IL FDD Act, but settlement agreements and arbitration are permitted
overrides.why_it_matters: Settlement of actual/potential claims is allowed; blanket waivers are not

overrides.override_summary: MD law may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: MD provides additional termination/renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: May sue in MD for claims arising under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law (3-year statute of limitations)
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees can litigate locally for MD law claims

overrides.override_summary: General release does not release franchisor from MD franchise law liability. No waiver of claims or disclaimer of reliance.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strongest anti-waiver language among state addenda

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause. 30-day cure opportunity required.
overrides.why_it_matters: MI law provides cure right for any lawful breach

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must fairly compensate on non-renewal (FMV of inventory/supplies/equipment/fixtures) if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing under different brand
overrides.why_it_matters: Only applies if term <5 years (Hilton's 23-year term may make this moot)

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration or litigation may not be required outside Michigan
overrides.why_it_matters: MI franchisees may refuse out-of-state litigation requirement

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require release that deprives franchisee of MI FIL rights (settlement of existing claims OK)
overrides.why_it_matters: Blanket releases of statutory rights void in MI

overrides.override_summary: 90-day notice of termination with 60-day cure period. 180-day non-renewal notice required.
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice/cure periods than FA provides (10–30 days)

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees cannot be required to consent to liquidated damages

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside MN, waiver of jury trial, or consent to termination penalties/judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees can litigate in MN with jury

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve from liability under MN Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MN statutory claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides statutory termination right not in FA

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchises Law governs any cause of action arising under NY Franchises Law. NY GBL §§680–695 may supersede inconsistent FA provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees get NY statutory protections even though FA already specifies NY law

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY courts may decline to enforce blanket releases

overrides.override_summary: ALL liquidated damages provisions deleted in their entirety
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees have no liquidated damages exposure — most protective state

overrides.override_summary: ND law supersedes NY law and FA provisions. FA governed by ND law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Complete override of choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Any venue/jurisdiction clause requiring forum outside ND is deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: All disputes may be heard in ND

overrides.override_summary: No release shall relieve from ND Franchise Disclosure Act liability
overrides.why_it_matters: ND statutory claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Release on transfer/renewal excludes RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI statutory claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Venue/choice of law restrictions void for RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can litigate locally for statutory claims

overrides.override_summary: Unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause (VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564)
overrides.why_it_matters: VA law provides additional protection against termination

overrides.override_summary: WA Franchise Investment Protection Act (RCW 19.100) prevails in event of conflict of laws
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law supersedes FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes WA Act rights except when executed pursuant to negotiated settlement after FA in effect with independent counsel
overrides.why_it_matters: High bar for enforceable release in WA

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed. Franchisee may bring action in WA.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisees can require WA venue

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete void unless employee earnings >$100K/year (adjusted for inflation) or contractor earnings >$250K/year
overrides.why_it_matters: Most restrictive noncompete override; earnings thresholds apply

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring employees of other franchisees or franchisor (RCW 49.62.060)
overrides.why_it_matters: No-poach/no-hire clauses void in WA
